assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable gives rise to foreign exchange exposure in the consolidated financial statements.
The relevant group entities have different functional currencies.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to changes in the fair value of a recognised asset or liability attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, including highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
